operating_state: Total revenue increased 2.4% to $21.9 billion driven by 6.3% U.S. growth offsetting 2.9% international decline and 1.8% currency headwinds. Innovative Medicine grew 2.3% to $13.9 billion with oncology advancing 20.4% operationally led by CARVYKTI (135% growth) and DARZALEX (20% growth), while STELARA declined 34% from biosimilar competition. MedTech increased 2.5% to $8.0 billion with cardiovascular up 16.4% including Shockwave acquisition contribution, offsetting 4.2% orthopaedics decline.
cost_structure: Gross margin compressed to 66.4% from 69.6% due to unfavorable product mix, currency impacts, and Shockwave inventory step-up charges. Operating expenses improved with selling and administrative costs declining to 23.3% of sales from 24.6% and research and development falling to 14.7% from 16.6%, reflecting operational leverage and prior restructuring benefits partially offset by acquisition integration costs.
material_events: Company reversed approximately $7.0 billion in talc reserves following Texas Bankruptcy Court dismissal of Red River case on March 31, 2025. Completed $14.6 billion Intra-Cellular Therapies acquisition in April funded by $9.2 billion senior unsecured notes issuance and €4 billion euro-denominated debt offering in February. Board increased quarterly dividend to $1.30 per share from $1.24 while executing $2.1 billion in share repurchases.
macro_exposures: STELARA biosimilar launches reduced operational sales by 4.7% with continued European and U.S. rollouts expected throughout 2025. Medicare Part D redesign impacted multiple products including ERLEADA, IMBRUVICA, and TREMFYA. China volume-based procurement pressures affected Surgery and Orthopaedics franchises while currency translation created $575 million negative impact in comprehensive income.
forward_outlook: Management expects continued oncology momentum with CARVYKTI and DARZALEX driving growth while STELARA biosimilar pressure persists. Intra-Cellular acquisition expands neuroscience capabilities with CAPLYTA integration. Orthopaedics restructuring program targeting $0.7-0.8 billion total costs through fiscal 2025 completion. Strong liquidity position of $38.8 billion supports strategic investments and remaining talc settlement obligations.
uncertainty_profile: Talc litigation exposure remains unresolved with approximately 62,850 plaintiffs following bankruptcy dismissal, requiring alternative resolution pathways. Patent challenges continue across XARELTO, INVEGA SUSTENNA, and CAPLYTA through federal courts and USPTO proceedings. DOJ investigations of Vision intraocular lens practices and Janssen REMICADE contracting create ongoing regulatory uncertainty. IRA Medicare Drug Price Negotiation constitutional challenge remains under appeal with uncertain implementation impact.
